rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE FINLEY VS. MITCHELL HUANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 24, 2019 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT COURTNEY DAVIS, PARK NORTH REAL ESTATE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for indemnity and contribution for Plaintiff to specify by page and line number claims asserted by Plaintiff in the First Amended Complaint alleged against Pacific Union/Hatvany in their alleged capacity as buyer's agent/broker (as opposed to their capacity as seller's agent/broker), which are now being re-asserted against the cross-defendants; and (2) if Pacific Union/Hatvany asserts that seller's agent/broker is entitled to indemnity by buyer's agent/broker, allege facts in support of elements required to state such a claim (case authority with similar factual posture, i.e. non-disclosure/concealment of material facts by buyer's agent/broker should be located and provided to Cross-Defendants in the next round of good faith meet and confer, if necessary). As to the cause of action for declaratory relief, the Court has previously ordered Cross-Complaint to allege only non-duplicative causes of action. Cross-Complainants concede that their declaratory relief cause of action is an indemnity cause of action. (Opposition 12:15-16), and therefore is duplicative. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK VS. RENKA PROP. LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For: (1) Terminating Sanctions, (2) Striking Defendants Answer, (3) For Entry Of Default Against Defendant, And (4) For Monetary Sanctions

PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK Notice Of Motion And Motion For: (1) Terminating Sanctions, (2) Striking Defendants Answer, (3) For Entry Of Default Against Defendant, And (4) For Monetary Sanctions Hearing Required. Counsel for Plaintiff and Defendant Renka Prop. LLC to appear in person. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JACLYN MCGLYNN VS. 634 POWELL I5, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Housing Rights Committee Of San Francisco And Brad Hirns Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 24, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT 634 POWELL I5, LLC, YAT-PANG AU, VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC., GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Housing Rights Committee Of San Francisco And Brad Hirns Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. HRC shall produce all documents directly relevant to the issues involved in this matter, except for (1) other documents covered by privacy; and (2) attorney-client privilege. As to those documents that are withheld due to privacy (except those directly relevant) and attorney-client privilege, a detailed privilege log must be provided to allow the parties (and the Court if necessary) to determine whether the privilege is properly claimed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JACLYN MCGLYNN VS. 634 POWELL I5, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses By Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 24, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT 634 POWELL I5, LLC, YAT-PANG AU, VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC., GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses By Plaintiffs Hearing Required. In light of judge Pro Tem James H. Fleming's report and recommendation, parties by no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 24, 2019 submit joint Separate Statement (for each type of discovery at issue and for each set of Plaintiffs based on representation) outlining remaining disputes. Parties to appear at 8:30 a.m. on July 24, 2019 and engage in good faith meet and confer efforts regarding outstanding discovery disputes subject to this motion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: AMY L. HUNT VS. MARCO SALCISSIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 24, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANTs AMY ONG and MARCO SALSICCIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to attach a complete copy of the lease. Demurrer is otherwise overruled: notice states a proposed date of termination in accordance with CC 1941.6 and complies with SFRO 37.9(i)(4) and (j)(3). = (501/CFH)
